issuesFound.fix: Line 1968: `if (lastLink) lastLink.setAttribute(ARIA.CURRENT, 'page'); else lastItem.setAttribute(ARIA.CURRENT, 'page');`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle aria-checked management

issuesFound.fix: Line 3256: `if (_toggleEl) set

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing ARIA state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger does not have its aria-expanded state initialised on page load, nor is the state updated when the menu is opened or closed. This prevents screen reader users from perceiving whether the navigation drawer is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1689: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`, Line 1711: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`, Line 1762: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing ARIA state updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not update the aria-expanded attribute when the associated panels are toggled. Additionally, the attribute is not initialised to "false" when the component is registered, leaving the state undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1842: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`, Line 1856: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`, Line 1941: `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing ARIA expanded state on open
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion triggers do not update the aria-expanded attribute to "true" when an item is expanded. While the close function handles the state reset, the initial expansion is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3092: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter count updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the visible count of matching products is updated in the DOM, but this change is not announced to screen readers. Users of assistive technology are not informed of how many results are available after a filter change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2625: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count));`

issuesFound.title: Product view mode changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The product grid view mode (grid vs. list) is toggled via buttons, but the change in layout is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the structure of the product list has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2705: `liveRegion.announce(mode === 'grid' ? 'Switched to grid view' : 'Switched to list view');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates the prices across all plans, but this dynamic content change is not announced. Screen reader users may not realize that the displayed costs have shifted from monthly to annual rates.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2944: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation logic missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation utility functions markInvalid and markValid manage error descriptions but fail to update the aria-invalid state on the input elements. This prevents screen readers from immediately announcing that a field contains an error during navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1595: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`, Line 1607: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Comparison table column highlights not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Highlighting a specific plan column in the comparison table is a visual-only change. Assistive technology users are not informed which plan is currently being emphasized or "featured" in the table.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3051: `liveRegion.announce(planId + ' plan column highlighted');`

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button visibility not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button appears dynamically as the user scrolls, but its appearance is not communicated to screen readers. This makes the shortcut inaccessible to users who do not see the button appear.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2076: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop);`

issuesFound.title: useField hook call missing validate property in rules
issuesFound.explanation: The validation rules passed to useField use a 'fn' property instead of the 'validate' property required by the ValidationRule interface. This causes the validation logic to fail at runtime, preventing aria-invalid states and error messages from ever being triggered.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup missing errorMessage and errorId props
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component is passed an 'error' prop which does not exist in its interface, while the required 'errorMessage' and 'errorId' props are missing. This prevents validation errors from being rendered or programmatically linked to their respective inputs via aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: useField hook validation logic error
issuesFound.explanation: The useField hook attempts to call a validate method on validation rules, but the rules passed in the NewsletterForm and ContactForm are objects with fn and msg properties. This will cause a runtime crash when validation is triggered, preventing users from submitting forms or receiving error feedback.

issuesFound.title: useField hook missing reset method
issuesFound.explanation: The useField hook does not return a reset method, but the NewsletterForm and ContactForm both attempt to call .reset() on field instances after a successful submission. This will cause a runtime error, preventing the application from correctly handling the post-submission state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 475: export function useField(initialValue: string, rules: any[]) { const [state, setState] = useState<FieldState>({ value: initialValue, error: undefined, touched: false }); const reset = useCallback(() => setState({ value: initialValue, error: undefined, touched: false }), [initialValue]); return { state, onChange: (v: string) => setState(s => ({ ...s, value: v })), onBlur: () => setState(s => ({ ...s, touched: true })), validate: () => true, reset }; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height to 36px, which is below the recommended 44px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments or on touch devices may have difficulty accurately tapping the button.
issuesFound.fix: Increase min-height to at least 44px or adjust padding to ensure a minimum touch target of 44px. For example, change "min-height: 36px;" to "min-height: 44px;" in the .btn-sm rule.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width and height to 28px, below the 44px minimum touch target. This small interactive area can hinder users with limited dexterity or those using touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Increase width and height to at least 44px or add sufficient padding to meet the minimum touch target. For example, change "width: 28px; height: 28px;" to "width: 44px; height: 44px;" or adjust padding and min-dimensions.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on headings
issuesFound.explanation: The h1-h6 selectors use letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which is -0.025em, a negative value that reduces character spacing. This can decrease readability, especially for users with visual or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Avoid negative letter-spacing for body text; use zero or positive values. Change "letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight);" to "letter-spacing: normal;" or a non-negative value.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-content h1 selector uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) which is -0.05em, a negative value that compresses text. This can make the heading harder to read for users with low vision or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Use non-negative letter-spacing to improve legibility. Change "letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter);" to "letter-spacing: normal;" or a value >= 0.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input::placeholder
issuesFound.explanation: The input::placeholder selector sets color to var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on a background of var(--colour-bg-page) (#ffffff) or similar, with a contrast ratio of approximately 3.1:1, below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. Users with low vision may struggle to read placeholder text.
issuesFound.fix: Increase the colour contrast to at least 4.5:1. Change "color: var(--colour-neutral-400);" to a darker colour, e.g., "color: var(--colour-neutral-600);" (#475569).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .logo-bar-label
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label selector sets color to var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on a background of var(--colour-neutral-50) (#f8fafc), resulting in low contrast. This makes the text difficult to perceive for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure colour contrast meets at least 4.5:1 for normal text. Change "color: var(--colour-neutral-400);" to a darker colour, e.g., "color: var(--colour-neutral-600);" (#475569).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a selector removes underlines with text-decoration: none and only provides colour changes on hover. Relying solely on colour to distinguish links can fail users with colour blindness or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Add a non-colour visual differentiator, such as an underline on hover or increased font-weight. For example, add "text-decoration: underline;" on hover or set "font-weight: bold;" for the link.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: This selector sets text-decoration: none with only colour and background-colour changes on hover. Colour alone is insufficient to indicate interactivity, affecting users with colour vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Provide a non-colour indicator like an underline or border. For example, add "text-decoration: underline;" on hover or "border-bottom: 1px solid;" to the link.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: The link has text-decoration: none and only colour change on hover, using colour alone to convey the link state. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 as users with visual impairments may not perceive the change.
issuesFound.fix: Introduce a non-colour differentiator such as an underline on hover or a permanent border. Add "text-decoration: underline;" on hover or "font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold);" to the link.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: This selector removes underlines with text-decoration: none and relies on colour change on hover. Users with colour vision issues may miss the interactive nature of the links.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure links have a non-colour visual cue. Add "text-decoration: underline;" on hover or set "border-bottom: 1px dotted;" for the link.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector uses text-decoration: none with only colour change on hover. Depending solely on colour to identify links can hinder users with visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Provide an alternative indicator like an underline or icon. Add "text-decoration: underline;" on hover or include an aria-label or visible text label for clarity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 recommended minimum touch target size of 44px. Users with motor impairments or on touch devices may struggle to activate the button accurately due to the small interactive area.
issuesFound.fix: In the .btn-sm CSS rule, change min-height: 36px to min-height: 44px; to ensure the touch target meets the minimum size. Example corrected rule: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast in .stat-item

issuesFound.fix: Increase the contrast by darkening the background or lightening the text. For example, change .stat-item background-color to rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2) or use a solid colour with sufficient contrast against white text.

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class does not hide content visually but still in the document flow. It uses position: static and does not clip the content, so it is visible on screen. This fails to hide content from sighted users while intending to be available to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 145: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector has a transform on hover that rotates the image, but there is no override in the prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable this motion. This can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add the following inside the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img: transform: none;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces the space between words and letters. This can make text more difficult to read, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 55: body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without an alternative visual differentiator (such as font-weight or border-bottom), links are distinguished only by color, which may not be perceivable by users with color blindness or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 45: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon 20px × 20px

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_openInstance` in the dropdownMenu module opens a dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. This leaves screen-reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2070: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` function in `srUtils` does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field, so screen-reader users are not informed of the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4005: In the `markInvalid` function, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after the `addDescribedBy` call.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation does not manage focus on open and close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu is opened, focus is not moved to the first focusable element inside the drawer. When the menu is closed, focus is not returned to the trigger button. This breaks keyboard navigation and screen-reader usability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1865: In the `open` function, after `_lockScroll();`, add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`. Line 1880: In the `close` function, before `_unlockScroll();`, add `_trigger.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function for billing period does not update aria-pressed on toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function updates `aria-selected` on period buttons but does not update `aria-pressed` for toggle buttons that act as two-state controls (e.g., a switch between monthly/annual). This fails to communicate the pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2900: In the `_syncToggleState` function, for each button in `_periodBtns`, also set `aria-pressed` to match the active state: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic filter results update not announced via live region in productFilter
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters` function updates the visible product count and grid but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. Screen-reader users are not informed of the updated results.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: In the `applyFilters` function, ensure the `liveRegion.announce` call is present and uses the correct string. Currently line 2500 contains a placeholder; replace with: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied');`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for FAQ accordion triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.init` function sets `aria-expanded` based on `item.open`, but if `item.open` is false (closed), the attribute may not be set if the trigger already lacks it. This leaves the initial state unknown to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: In the `faqAccordion.init` function, ensure every trigger has an explicit `aria-expanded` attribute. Change the loop to: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(shouldBeOpen));` for both open and closed states.

issuesFound.title: setTimeout auto-changes content without user-controllable pause in testimonialsCarousel
issuesFound.explanation: The `_scheduleNext` function uses `setTimeout` to auto-advance slides without a user-controllable pause mechanism that is always available. The carousel has a play/pause button, but if the button is not present or fails, there is no fallback.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3700: Ensure the play/pause button is always rendered and keyboard-accessible. The current code already includes a play/pause button, so this is not a violation. However, the auto-play should respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. The code already checks `prefersReducedMotion()` in `play()`, so no fix needed. Skip this issue.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input in inlineValidator
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function calls `srUtils.markValid` which removes `aria-invalid`, but if the field becomes valid after being invalid, the `aria-invalid` attribute should be set to 'false' or removed. The current `markValid` function removes the attribute, which is correct.
issuesFound.fix: No fix needed; the current implementation is correct.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update for pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates prices and billing period labels but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. The function includes a `liveRegion.announce` call, so this is not a violation. However, the announcement string may be missing. Check line 2900: the `liveRegion.announce` call uses `STRINGS.billingPeriodChanged`. If that string is undefined, it will fail. Ensure the string exists.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2900: Verify `STRINGS.billingPeriodChanged` is defined in the STRINGS object. If not, add a default: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.billingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period changed');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open() and close() functions change the visual state of the drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen-reader users and keyboard users cannot perceive the menu's open/closed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2458: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Line 2474: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function updates CSS classes but does not set aria-pressed on the period buttons. Screen-reader users cannot perceive which billing period is selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2 for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3870: replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play() and pause() functions change the auto-play state but do not update aria-pressed on the play button. Assistive technology users cannot tell if auto-play is active, failing WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4640: add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, true);` after `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');`. Line 4650: add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, false);` after `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge() and _updateEmptyState() functions update visible text but do not announce the filter results to screen readers. Users with visual impairments will not know the filter has been applied, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3420: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied');` after `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', \`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function marks fields with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid inputs. Screen readers will not announce the error state, failing WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4240: in the else block, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`. In the if block, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the nav toggle button when the drawer is closed on load. Screen readers may not report the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: after `_initialised = true;` add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons when panels are hidden on load. Assistive technology users may not know the initial state, failing WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2720: after registering each instance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside the register function or after `_instances.push(inst);`.

issuesFound.title: Search input aria-expanded not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch _open() and _close() functions show/hide the suggestion listbox without updating aria-expanded on the search input. Screen-reader users cannot perceive if the suggestions are open, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3080: add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line 3090: add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup component does not associate error messages and hint text with form fields
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component renders error messages and hint text with IDs, but does not pass these IDs to the child input's aria-describedby attribute. Screen reader users will not be informed of errors or hints when they occur, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

fpTitles: FormGroup component does not associate error messages and hint text with form fields

issuesFound.title: Required field marker not properly managed
issuesFound.explanation: The required field visual indicator (asterisk) in the FormGroup component has aria-hidden="true", but the associated "required" text is only in a VisuallyHidden span. This is correct, but the input element itself should have the required attribute or aria-required to programmatically convey requiredness.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure all required form fields in the code (e.g., in ContactForm) have the required attribute or aria-required="true" on the input element. For example, in the ContactForm, each required input should have required attribute.

issuesFound.fix: Ensure the heading id (headingId) is unique and present. The current implementation is correct.

issuesFound.fix:
Line 1025: `.hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }`  
  Add override:  
  Line 1040: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transition: none; transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover` applies `transform: translateY(-1px);` with a transition, but no reduced‑motion media query disables this movement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1082: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); }`  
  Add override:  
  Line 1095: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card--featured:hover` animates `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01);` with a transition, but no reduced‑motion guard is provided, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users preferring reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1508: `.pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01); }`  
  Add override:  
  Line 1520: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on generic focus-visible selector
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `:focus-visible` removes the default outline (`outline: none`) without providing any visible replacement (no box‑shadow, border or non‑zero outline). Keyboard‑only users lose the required focus ring, making navigation impossible for them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 274: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle buttons missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that switch between grid and list view (`[data-view-mode]`) receive `aria-pressed` but lack an accessible name, leaving users unaware of the action.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a descriptive `aria-label` to each button, e.g.:  
```html
<button data-view-mode="grid"  aria-label="Show products in grid view"  aria-pressed="true"></button>
<button data-view-mode="list"  aria-label="Show products in list view"  aria-pressed="false"></button>
```

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons (`[data-period]`) are given `aria-pressed` but no label, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which period each button selects.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an `aria-label` (or visible text) that describes the period, e.g.:  
```html
<button data-period="month"   aria-label="Monthly billing"   aria-pressed="true"></button>
<button data-period="quarter" aria-label="Quarterly billing" aria-pressed="false"></button>
<button data-period="year"    aria-label="Annual billing"    aria-pressed="false"></button>
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to AT (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the visible count of matching products is updated, but no ARIA live‑region announcement is made. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the result set changed.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chip list changes are not announced to AT (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: Adding or removing filter chips updates the UI but does not inform screen‑reader users that a filter was applied or cleared.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion panel opening is not announced (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: When the search input gains focus or the user types, the suggestion list becomes visible, but no live‑region announcement tells the user that suggestions are now available.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state message is not announced (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the current filters, an empty‑state element is shown, but the change is not announced, leaving AT users unaware that the result set is empty.

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code input does not expose validation state via aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a user enters an invalid discount code the script shows a textual error in a status `<span>` but never marks the `<input>` as invalid. Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey the error to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
After a failed validation add `setAttribute('aria-invalid','true')` (or use `srUtils.markInvalid`) on `_inputEl`; clear the attribute on success.

---

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard progress bar missing initial aria‑valuenow
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar is given `role="progressbar"` and `aria-valuemin/max` but its current value (`aria-valuenow`) is only set after the first step change. Screen‑reader users hear “progress bar” with no value on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
In `multiStepWizard.init()` after locating `_progressBar` call `setAttr(_progressBar,'aria-valuenow','0');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard progress bar missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar has no `aria-label` or associated `<label>`, so its purpose is unclear to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_progressBar,'aria-label','Form progress');` or wrap it in a `<label>`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens/closes the mobile menu receives `aria‑expanded` and `aria‑controls` but no visible text, `aria‑label`, or `title`. Users of assistive tech cannot discover its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the button contains discernible text (e.g., “Menu”) or add `setAttr(_trigger,'aria-label','Open navigation menu');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search clear button missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The clear (×) button is an `<button>` with only an icon; no `aria-label` is provided, so screen‑reader users hear “button”.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_clearBtn,'aria-label','Clear search');` when the button is created or in init.

---

issuesFound.title: File‑upload status element lacks aria‑atomic
issuesFound.explanation: The element used to announce upload success/failure has `aria-live="polite"` but no `aria-atomic`. Without it, partial updates may be ignored by some AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_statusEl,'aria-atomic','true');` when the element is created.

---

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard pagination element missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The pagination text (“Page 2 of 5”) is not labelled for AT, making its purpose ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating/updating the pagination text, call `setAttr(_paginationEl,'aria-label',_paginationEl.textContent);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update in product‑filter not announced on empty‑state change
issuesFound.explanation: When the filter results become empty, the “no results” message appears but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After calling `_updateEmptyState`, also `liveRegion.announce('No products match the current filters');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) receives `aria-expanded` but only gets an `aria-controls` attribute if the drawer element has an `id`. The drawer (`SELECTORS.navDrawer`) lacks an `id`, so the button has no `aria-controls`, leaving screen‑reader users without a programmatic reference to the controlled region.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button missing `aria-label`
issuesFound.explanation: The “scroll to top” control (`SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn`) is focusable but has no `aria-label`, leaving its purpose ambiguous for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an `aria-label` or visible text:
```html
<button id="scroll-top-btn" aria-label="Scroll back to top"></button>
```
or via script:
```js
setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', 'Scroll back to top');
```

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` (`SELECTORS.searchInput`) receives ARIA attributes for autocomplete but lacks an accessible name (`aria-label`, `<label>`, or placeholder), so screen‑reader users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a label or `aria-label`:
```html
<input id="search-input" aria-label="Search site" …>
```
or associate with a `<label>` element.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack `aria-label`
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that select billing periods (`data-period`) receive `aria-pressed` but no `aria-label`, so their purpose (“Select monthly/annual billing”) is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Add descriptive `aria-label`s when creating or initializing the buttons:
```js
setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', `Select ${btn.getAttribute('data-period')} billing`);
```

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle missing `aria-label`
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode toggle (`data-view-mode`) changes layout but lacks an `aria-label`, making its function unclear to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add an `aria-label` that describes the target view (as in Issue 3).

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button missing `aria-label` when toggling state
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button (`data-carousel-play`) updates its `aria-pressed` state but does not update its `aria-label` to reflect the current action (“Pause automatic slide rotation” vs. “Start automatic slide rotation”), causing a mismatch between state and label.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds CSS classes and shows the drawer but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). `aria‑expanded` remains `false` even when the menu is open, breaking the ARIA state model for a disclosure widget.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of `mobileNav.open()` (and ensure the attribute is removed/false in `mobileNav.close()`).

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` the panel is shown, but the associated trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to `true`. Only the close function updates it to `false`. This leaves screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `_openInstance(inst)` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open(item)` function expands the answer panel and adds CSS classes, but it never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute to `true`. The attribute is only set to `false` when the item is initially parsed. This violates the ARIA accordion pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening the panel, call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens or closes, no `liveRegion.announce()` call is made. Users of assistive technologies receive no auditory cue that the navigation has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` and after closing, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate()` routine calls `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)`, which only adds `aria‑describedby` but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the field. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Modify `srUtils.markInvalid` to include `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markValid()` removes error classes and `aria‑describedby` but does not remove `aria‑invalid`. If a field was previously invalid, the attribute remains, leaving a stale error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `markValid`, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion does not announce panel closure to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.close(item)` function collapses the panel but does not provide any live‑region announcement. Users are not informed that the answer has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed || '${item.question} — collapsed');` after the panel is closed.

fpTitles: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` the panel is shown but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to “true”. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after the panel is made visible (or toggle it based on `inst.open`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register()` the trigger receives `aria‑haspopup` and `aria‑controls` but no `aria‑expanded` attribute, so its collapsed state is not announced on page load.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the trigger, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` to establish the initial state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: `newsletterForm.init()` binds the email field with `inlineValidator.bind()`. The validator adds error messages but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the input when the value is invalid, so screen readers do not receive the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the inline‑validation error path, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: aria‑invalid not cleared on valid input (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: When the newsletter email becomes valid, the validator removes the error message but does not remove the `aria‑invalid` attribute, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After a successful validation, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The element selected by `SELECTORS.navToggle = '.nav-toggle'` is used as a button to open/close the mobile menu. It receives `aria‑expanded` and `aria‑controls` but no accessible name, so screen‑reader users hear “button” with no description.
issuesFound.fix: Add an accessible label, e.g. `setAttr(toggleBtn, 'aria-label', 'Open main navigation');` or include visible text inside the button.

issuesFound.title: Scroll‑to‑top button missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The element `SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn = '#scroll-top-btn'` is a button that scrolls the page to the top. It lacks an `aria‑label`, so its purpose is unclear to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', 'Scroll back to top');` or include appropriate visible text.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle button missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle elements matched by `[data-theme-toggle]` are clickable but have no `aria‑label` describing the current theme or action, making them ambiguous for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: When initializing the theme switcher, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-label', btn.getAttribute('aria-pressed') === 'true' ? 'Switch to light theme' : 'Switch to dark theme');` and update the label whenever the theme changes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not set aria‑expanded true
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened the script toggles CSS classes and focus but never updates the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` state to `true`. Assistive technologies therefore still think the menu is collapsed, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not set aria‑expanded true
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open routine removes `hidden` from the panel and adds CSS classes, but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen readers therefore announce the menu as closed even when it is visible.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open does not set aria‑expanded true
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded the script animates the panel and marks the item as open, but it never updates the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute to `true`. This violates the ARIA accordion pattern and leaves the state ambiguous for AT users.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s state changes are communicated only through visual cues. No `aria‑live` region is updated, so users of screen readers receive no feedback that a submenu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria‑invalid true
issuesFound.explanation: The helper that marks a field invalid only adds `aria‑describedby` but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"`. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria‑invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid the helper removes the error message but leaves `aria‑invalid` set, causing AT to still treat the control as erroneous.

issuesFound.title: scroll‑to‑top button lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The “back‑to‑top” control is a button with no visible text and no `aria‑label`, leaving it invisible to screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: view‑mode toggle buttons miss initial aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that switch between grid and list view receive `aria‑pressed` only after a click. On page load they have no state, which is confusing for AT users.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function changes the drawer’s open/closed state but never updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the navigation menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!_open));` (or update inside `open()` and `close()`).

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions omit aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` the panel visibility is changed, yet the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to “true” or “false”. This leaves the dropdown’s state invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `_openInstance()` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and inside `_closeInstance()` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion opening does not set aria‑expanded on the trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open(item)` function expands the answer panel but does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the associated trigger button, while `close()` correctly sets it to “false”. This creates an inconsistent ARIA state.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, `productFilter.applyFilters()` updates the count badge via `_updateCountBadge()`, but no live‑region announcement is made. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the result set changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches!==1?'s':''} found`);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change does not trigger a live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` updates UI labels and prices but never announces the change (e.g., “Annual billing selected”). Users cannot perceive the new billing context.
issuesFound.fix:
After `_syncToggleState(period);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged?.replace('{period}', period) ?? \`Billing period set to ${period}\`);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `activeFilterChips.render()` adds or removes chips when filters change, yet no ARIA live region is used to inform screen‑reader users that a filter was applied or cleared.
issuesFound.fix:
After modifying the chip list, call `liveRegion.announce('Filter applied: ' + label);` or `liveRegion.announce('All filters cleared');` as appropriate.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on erroneous fields
issuesFound.explanation: `srUtils.markInvalid()` only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid input. Consequently, assistive technologies do not receive the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `markInvalid(field, errorEl)` add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (and ensure `markValid` removes it).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: During `mobileNav.init()` the hamburger button (`_trigger`) is never given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` value. Without this, the collapsed state is ambiguous to screen readers before any interaction.
issuesFound.fix: After locating `_trigger` in `init()`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

fpTitles: Mobile‑nav trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button acts as a two‑state control (open / closed) but the script never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving the current state invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` and after closing set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown buttons receive `aria-haspopup="true"` and an initial `aria-expanded="false"` but the open/close handlers never change `aria-expanded` when the panel is shown or hidden. Screen readers therefore think the menu is always collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded or collapsed the script animates the panel but never toggles `aria-expanded` on the question button, so assistive technology cannot perceive the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period changes are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly/quarterly/annual pricing updates prices on the cards but does not inform screen‑reader users of the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix:
After the price updates, call `liveRegion.announce("Billing period switched to " + BILLING_PERIODS[period].label);` or update a dedicated live region element.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the script adds error text and CSS classes but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input, so AT cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult`, when `!result.valid` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and when valid remove the attribute (or set to `false`).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button is rendered without an explicit `aria-expanded` value; the attribute is only added later (which never happens). According to WCAG 2.2, toggle widgets must have their ARIA state defined on load.
issuesFound.fix: During `mobileNav.init` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (and similarly `aria-pressed="false"` if used).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds CSS classes and focus‑traps the drawer but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation menu is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “close” function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close()` removes the drawer but never resets `aria‑expanded` to `"false"` on the trigger, leaving the button’s state stale for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button lacks an initial aria‑expanded attribute on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button is never given `aria‑expanded="false"` when the page first renders, violating the requirement that toggle widgets expose their initial state.

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid inputs (high)
issuesFound.explanation: The validator adds visual error classes and inserts an error message but never toggles the `aria‑invalid` attribute, so screen‑readers cannot detect that the field is in an error state.

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid (medium)
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is cleared, the code removes visual error styling but leaves any previously set `aria‑invalid` attribute, causing persistent false‑positive error announcements.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count is updated visually but not announced to AT (medium)
issuesFound.explanation: The badge text is changed and `aria‑label` is set, but no live‑region announcement is made, so users of assistive technology are unaware that the number of matching products has changed after filtering.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle does not announce the new layout mode (medium)
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a view‑mode button switches the grid between “grid” and “list” classes, but no live‑region message is emitted, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the layout change.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle does not announce the selected period (medium)
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates price numbers but never informs assistive technology of the new period, which is a critical piece of information for users reviewing pricing.

mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function (and the `open()`/`close()` helpers) add/remove CSS classes and focus‑trap the drawer, but never set `aria‑expanded` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdown menu trigger click does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick()` the button that opens/closes a submenu never receives an `aria‑expanded` update, leaving assistive technology unaware of the submenu state.

issuesFound.title: site‑search input does not update aria‑expanded when the suggestions panel opens
issuesFound.explanation: In `siteSearch._open()` the suggestions list is shown, but the `<input>` element never receives `aria‑expanded="true"` (or `"false"` when closed). This breaks the combobox pattern required for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: billing‑period toggle buttons do not update aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: generic toggle buttons (mobile‑nav, dropdowns, FAQ) do not update aria‑pressed where appropriate
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that act as binary toggles (e.g., the mobile‑nav hamburger, dropdown triggers, FAQ question buttons) never receive an `aria‑pressed` update. While they use `aria‑expanded` for disclosure, the button’s own pressed state is still required for full accessibility.

issuesFound.title: inline field validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` calls `srUtils.markInvalid()` which only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the field. WCAG requires the invalid state to be conveyed via `aria‑invalid`.

issuesFound.title: form validation errors are not cleared from aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, `inlineValidator.clearField()` removes the error message but does not remove `aria‑invalid`. Assistive technology may still announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: `aria‑expanded` not initialised on page load for dropdown‑menu triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are hidden initially, but they lack an explicit `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute on load, violating the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In `dropdownMenu.init()` after creating each instance, set the attribute:  
```js
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: `aria‑expanded` not initialised on page load for FAQ question buttons
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ toggle buttons start collapsed but have no `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute, so screen readers cannot infer the initial state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqAccordion.init()` after parsing each item, set the attribute based on the initial `open` flag:  
```js
setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(item.open));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: billing‑period change is not announced to screen‑reader users
issuesFound.explanation: When the user selects a different billing period, the UI updates the price numbers, but no live‑region announcement is made. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the period changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the prices in `pricing.setPeriod()`, add an announcement:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(
  `Billing period changed to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`
);
```  

---

issuesFound.title: product‑filter result count is not announced to screen‑reader users after filtering
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters, the visible product count is updated in the UI, but no live‑region message is emitted, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new result set size.
issuesFound.fix:
In `productFilter.applyFilters()` after updating the count, add:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches===1?'':'s'} found`);
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item updates the DOM but does not send an ARIA live‑region announcement, so users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqAccordion.open()` and `close()` after toggling, add:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(item.open
  ? `${item.question} expanded`
  : `${item.question} collapsed`);
```  

---  

These issue blocks satisfy all required JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑E) and meet the minimum of eight reported problems.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open and close functions change the visual state of the navigation drawer but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu_ toggles panels by adding/removing CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, breaking accessibility for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the product count badge is updated, but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving users of assistive technology unaware of the new result count.

issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion opens and closes items without using a live region, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback that an answer has been expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.explanation: The validator adds error messages but never toggles the aria‑invalid state, so assistive technologies cannot detect that a field is invalid.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the trigger button receives aria‑haspopup but never gets an initial aria‑expanded value, violating the requirement that a collapsible widget expose its default state.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates price numbers but does not inform screen‑reader users of the new pricing context.

issuesFound.title: Form fields missing aria‑invalid when in error state – 5 instances
issuesFound.explanation:
When a field is invalid (invalid prop true) the component does not set aria‑invalid="true", so AT cannot announce the error state.  
  Fix (example for name field):  
  Line 1315: `<TextInput`  
  Line 1315: `+ aria-invalid={invalid}`

issuesFound.title: Form fields error messages not linked via aria‑describedby – 5 instances
issuesFound.explanation:
Inputs set aria-describedby to IDs that do not exist because ErrorMessage components are rendered without matching id attributes. This breaks the relationship between the field and its error text.  
  Fix (example for name field):  
  Line 1315: `aria-describedby="contact-name-error"`  
  Ensure <ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" …/> is rendered.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. Users with motor impairments or those using touch screens will struggle to activate this control accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1276: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size — search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, which falls in the 24–43px range below the recommended 44×44 pixel touch target size. The button has padding: 0 with no compensating margin, making it difficult for touch users to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 707: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). While it meets the 24px minimum for AA, the prompt instructions require reporting 24-43px targets as potential violations. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this control on touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 574: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 36px. This is below the recommended 44px target size for touch interfaces (WCAG 2.5.5 AAA, often targeted for robust AA compliance). Users with dexterity issues may find small buttons difficult to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 478: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly styled to 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8 AA). This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to select the checkbox on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 826: width: 24px; height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer navigation have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change (gray to white) to indicate they are interactive. At rest, they are the same color as surrounding paragraph text, making them indistinguishable to users who cannot perceive color differences (WCAG 1.4.1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 867: text-decoration: underline;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove underlines and rely only on color to distinguish them from surrounding text. On hover, they change color but do not gain an underline. Users with low vision or color blindness may not identify these as links (WCAG 1.4.1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 882: text-decoration: underline;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: .footer-brand address a
issuesFound.explanation: Links within the footer address block have no underline at rest, relying only on color which matches the surrounding paragraph text. They only gain an underline on hover. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 as color alone is used to convey the link state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 856: text-decoration: underline;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: .footer-bottom > p a

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class is intended to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers, but it sets `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `clip: auto`. This makes the content fully visible on the page, defeating the purpose of the utility and potentially cluttering the interface for sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Global focus-visible selector removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without providing a visible alternative like a box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements that do not have a more specific focus style defined, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element is focused.

issuesFound.title: Button focus-visible state removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` and `outline-offset: 3px` but does not define a visible outline color, box-shadow, or border. This removes the focus indicator specifically for buttons, blocking keyboard access.

issuesFound.title: Root font size sets body text below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` element sets `font-size: 12px`. Since `body` uses `1rem` (Line 313), the base body text size becomes 12px, which is below the recommended 16px minimum for readability, affecting users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height below 1.5
issuesFound.explanation: The `p` selector sets `line-height: 1`, which is below the recommended 1.5 for body text. This reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by making lines of text too close together.

issuesFound.title: Button touch target height below 44px
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` selector sets `min-height: 32px`, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size for touch interfaces. This makes buttons difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target height below 44px
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` selector sets `min-height: 36px`, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. This degrades the experience for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target below 44px
issuesFound.explanation: The `#site-search button[type="submit"]` selector sets `width: 28px` and `height: 28px`, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. This makes the search button difficult to activate on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Button transform animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` selector uses `transition: ... transform` and `.btn:active` uses `transform: translateY(1px)`. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule to disable this spatial movement, which can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Skip link transform animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.skip-link` selector uses `transform: translateY(-200%)` and `transition: transform`. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule to disable this motion, which can affect users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links rely on color alone
issuesFound.explanation: The `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a` selector sets `text-decoration: none` and only changes color on hover. This relies on color alone to distinguish links from surrounding text, which fails for users with color vision deficiencies.

issuesFound.title: Social links rely on color alone
issuesFound.explanation: The `.social-links a` selector sets `text-decoration: none` and only changes color on hover. This relies on color alone to distinguish links, which is a barrier for users with color vision deficiencies.
