issuesFound.fix: Line 1585-1586: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove outline on focus-visible without visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on checkbox controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1593-1594: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot track focus through footer navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1617-1619: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove outline on focus-visible without visible alternative. Keyboard users lose focus visibility in footer menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1631-1633: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on social links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1641-1643: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has explicit height of 28px, below the 44px minimum touch target size recommended by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this field on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 663-672: #search-input { width: 220px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); padding-right: var(--space-10); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-50); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-full); transition: border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); appearance: none; -webkit-appearance: none; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, below the 44px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this button on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 687-693: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static instead of position: absolute, and lacks clip/clip-path properties. Content using this class will be visible on screen and not properly hidden from visual users, defeating the purpose of screen-reader-only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 416-425: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform transition for 3D rotation effect, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 735-739 contains empty rules. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the rotation animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735-739: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em which compresses space between words. This impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities, dyslexia, or low vision who benefit from increased letter and word spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 157: body { word-spacing: 0; letter-spacing: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links. Many links in the stylesheet rely solely on colour change to indicate they are interactive, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 173: a { text-decoration: underline; }

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements that do not have a more specific override, blocking keyboard navigation access.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users will not see which button is focused, violating WCAG 2.4.7.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Users navigating the footer via keyboard cannot identify the focused link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1968: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which leaves content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This exposes helper text to all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 473: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes a transform transition which causes spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disabling this specific animation, which may cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 613: .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }
  Line 1073: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text significantly harder to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: body { word-spacing: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section text
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.8:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 933: .section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin-top: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector uses --colour-neutral-400 (#94a3b8) on a dark background (--colour-neutral-900 #555555). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.5:1, making links unreadable for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1963: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. Links in body text (e.g., inside paragraphs) rely solely on colour to be distinguished from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 if colour perception is impaired.
issuesFound.fix: Line 253: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector sets min-height: 32px. This is below the recommended 44px minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8), making buttons difficult to activate for users with motor impairments on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 623: .btn { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px. This is below the absolute minimum 24px threshold (WCAG 2.5.8), making the control extremely difficult to select.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1931: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none. This removes the focus indicator specifically for users relying on High Contrast Mode, blocking navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 443: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField keyboard handler uses incorrect constant case
issuesFound.explanation: The keydown event listener for the file upload drop zone checks `KEYS.enter` and `KEYS.space`, but the `KEYS` constant object defines them as `ENTER` and `SPACE` (uppercase). This causes the condition to always evaluate to false, preventing keyboard users from activating the upload zone with Enter or Space keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3345: if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) {

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The `init()` function only calls `play()` or `pause()` (which set `aria-pressed` on `_playBtn`) if autoplay is enabled. If autoplay is disabled or reduced motion is preferred, the play/pause button remains without an `aria-pressed` attribute, leaving its state unknown to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3495: if ((CONFIG.carousel ? CONFIG.carousel.autoPlay !== false : true) && !prefersReducedMotion()) { play(); } else { pause(); }

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator dynamic text updates lack aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate()` function updates the `textContent` of stat elements to show counting numbers. This dynamic change conveys information but is not announced to screen readers because the element lacks an `aria-live` attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3830: el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite'); el.textContent = _format(val, cfg);

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent existing banner element missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When an existing cookie banner element is found in the DOM (the `else` block), the code fails to set `aria-modal="true"`. This attribute is required for dialog roles to inform assistive technology that content outside the banner is inert.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3740: } else { setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-modal', 'true'); qs('[data-cookie-accept]', _bannerEl) && ...

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard steps assigned role="tabpanel" without tablist
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard initialization assigns `role="tabpanel"` to step panels. This role requires a parent container with `role="tablist"` and corresponding tab elements to be valid. Without a tablist, this role is semantically incorrect and may confuse assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3695: step.panelEl.setAttribute('role', 'region');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions within the `dropdownMenu` module update the visual state and `aria-expanded` attribute but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Menu opened") to screen readers via a live region. This deprives non-visual users of confirmation that their interaction succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2325: function _openInstance(inst) { ... liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened'); }
  Line 2345: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed'); }

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation failure not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onApply` function in `discountCodeInput` displays an error message and announces it via `liveRegion` when validation fails, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the `_inputEl`. Screen readers relying on the invalid state property will not identify the field as erroneous.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3680: if (!result.valid) { _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation failure not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_addFile` function in `fileUploadField` announces validation errors via `liveRegion` when a file is rejected, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the `zoneEl` or `inputEl`. Assistive technologies cannot programmatically detect the error state on the drop zone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4360: if (!validation.valid) { zoneEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); liveRegion.announceAssertive(...); }

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator dynamic content updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function in `statCounterAnimator` updates the `textContent` of stat elements dynamically to count up numbers. The element lacks `aria-live="polite"`, so screen readers do not announce the changing values or the final result unless focus is already on the element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4790: el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite'); el.setAttribute('aria-atomic', 'true');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner appearance not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner` function in `cookieConsent` injects and displays the consent dialog and traps focus, but it does not announce "Cookie settings banner opened" via `liveRegion`. Users may be confused by the sudden focus trap without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4970: function _showBanner() { ... liveRegion.announce('Cookie settings banner opened. Please review our cookie policy.'); }

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader visual hide/show state not reflected in aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `hide` and `show` functions in `stickyHeader` toggle the `headerHidden` CSS class to visually hide the header, but they do not toggle `aria-hidden="true"` on the `_header` element. Screen readers may still navigate the hidden header content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2590: function hide() { ... _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); }
  Line 2605: function show() { ... _header.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); }

issuesFound.title: Deprecated aria-dropeffect attribute used in file upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria-dropeffect` attribute is deprecated in WAI-ARIA 1.1 and removed in 1.2. Screen readers ignore it, making it useless clutter that may confuse developers or validators. It is set on the `zoneEl` during drag events and initialization.

issuesFound.title: aria-roledescription used without valid base role on carousel root
issuesFound.explanation: `aria-roledescription` is used to override the accessible name of an element's role, but the `_rootEl` does not have a valid base `role` (e.g., `region` or `group`). Without a base role, `aria-roledescription` is ignored by assistive technologies, failing to convey the "carousel" context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4805: _rootEl.setAttribute('role', 'region'); _rootEl.setAttribute('aria-roledescription', 'carousel');

issuesFound.title: aria-roledescription used without valid base role on carousel slides
issuesFound.explanation: `aria-roledescription='slide'` is applied to slide elements (`el`) without a valid base `role` (e.g., `group` or `tabpanel`). Assistive technologies require a valid base role to interpret `aria-roledescription`, so this description is likely ignored.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4798: el.setAttribute('role', 'group'); el.setAttribute('aria-roledescription', 'slide');

issuesFound.title: Conflicting aria-live and role="alert" on inline validator error elements
issuesFound.explanation: The error element is given `role="alert"` (which implies `aria-live="assertive"`) but also explicitly set to `aria-live="polite"`. This conflict creates unpredictable behavior in screen readers, which may prioritize one attribute over the other inconsistently.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4895: role: 'alert'
Line 4896: remove 'aria-live': 'polite'

issuesFound.title: Conflicting aria-live and role="alert" on info toast notifications
issuesFound.explanation: Info toasts are assigned `role="alert"` (assertive) but the code explicitly sets `aria-live="polite"` for non-error types. This contradiction confuses assistive technologies about the urgency of the announcement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1985: role: type === 'error' ? 'alert' : 'status'
Line 1988: [ARIA.LIVE]: type === 'error' ? 'assertive' : 'polite'

issuesFound.title: Redundant and conflicting aria-pressed and aria-selected on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period buttons are assigned both `aria-pressed` (for toggle buttons) and `aria-selected` (for tabs/options). Depending on the element's actual role, one of these attributes is invalid, creating semantic noise for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3769: // Remove aria-pressed if role="tab", or remove aria-selected if role="button"
Line 3770: // Ensure only the state attribute matching the role is set

issuesFound.title: Invalid aria-selected attribute used on table header cells
issuesFound.explanation: `aria-selected` is applied to `<th>` elements (table headers) in the comparison highlighter. `aria-selected` is not a valid state for `columnheader` or `rowheader` roles, causing assistive technologies to ignore it or report an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3945: remove setAttr(th, 'aria-selected', 'true'); // Or change role to gridcell if interactive

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button uses invalid aria-checked attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element (`_toggleEl`) is assigned `aria-checked` in `_syncToggleState` (Line 2415). If the element is a native `<button>`, it retains the implicit `role="button"`, which does not support `aria-checked` (valid only on checkbox, switch, radio, etc.). The code only assigns `role="switch"` if the element is NOT a button (Line 2495), leaving native buttons with an invalid ARIA state attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: if (_toggleEl.tagName === 'BUTTON') { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); } else { setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear)); }

issuesFound.title: FAQ search input assigned invalid role="searchbox"
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.init`, the search input element is assigned `role="searchbox"`. This is not a valid role in WAI-ARIA 1.1/1.2 specifications (only `role="search"` exists for landmarks). This invalid role may confuse assistive technologies or be ignored, failing to provide the intended semantic information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: // Remove invalid role: input type="search" or standard label is sufficient. Remove this line: setAttr(_searchInputEl, 'role', 'searchbox');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu` module functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` update visibility and `aria-expanded` but do not call `liveRegion.announce` to inform screen reader users that the menu has opened or closed. Unlike other modules (e.g., `mobileNav`, `faqAccordion`), this dynamic state change is silent to AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1525: liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded'); Line 1545: liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');

issuesFound.title: Product filter view toggle buttons missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: In `productFilter.init`, the view mode toggle buttons (`[data-view-mode]`) have their CSS classes updated to reflect the initial state, but their `aria-pressed` attribute is not set. `aria-pressed` is only updated in the click handler (`_onViewToggle`). On page load, the ARIA state does not match the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1975: qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode)); });

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button missing aria-pressed on reduced motion
issuesFound.explanation: In `testimonialsCarousel.init`, if `prefersReducedMotion()` is true, the `play()` function is skipped. Consequently, `pause()` is also not called. The play/pause button (`_playBtn`) is never assigned an `aria-pressed` value, leaving its state undefined for screen readers when animations are disabled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3035: if (...) { play(); } else { pause(); }

issuesFound.title: FAQ item container ID not set for deep linking
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqRegistry.parse`, a unique `idAttr` is generated if the FAQ item container (`el`) lacks an ID. However, this ID is stored in the `item` object but never assigned back to `el.id`. When `faqAccordion.open` attempts to update the URL hash using `item.el.id` (Line 2680), it fails if the element didn't have an ID initially, breaking deep linking.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2585: if (!el.id) el.id = idAttr;

issuesFound.title: File upload zone uses deprecated aria-dropeffect attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In `fileUploadField.bind`, the drop zone element is assigned `aria-dropeffect="copy"` and `aria-dropeffect="none"`. This attribute was deprecated in WAI-ARIA 1.1 and removed in 1.2. Using deprecated ARIA attributes is a best-practice violation and may be ignored by modern browsers/AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3945: // Remove deprecated attribute. Line 3950: // Remove deprecated attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay missing aria-hidden when decorative
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.open`, the overlay element (`_overlay`) is made visible but is not assigned `aria-hidden="true"`. If the overlay is purely decorative (visual click target handled by document listener) and contains no text, it should be hidden from assistive technologies to prevent it from being perceived as interactive content or blocking focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1335: _overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds classes and announces the state change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not know the menu is open, as only the close() function resets the attribute to false.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function removes the hidden attribute and adds classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. This leaves the trigger's state inconsistent with the visible panel, confusing assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in faqAccordion expands the panel and announces the change but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating a state mismatch.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field itself. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to identify the field as erroneous before reading the description.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the change is visible but not announced to screen readers. WCAG 4.1.3 requires status messages to be available to assistive technology, specifically for dynamic content like menu states.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, the state change is not announced. Users relying on screen readers are not informed that the menu has collapsed, potentially leaving them confused about the current interface state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function announces the expansion, but the close function does not announce the collapse. This inconsistency prevents screen reader users from tracking the state of accordion items reliably.

issuesFound.title: Discount input validation fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onApply` function in `discountCodeInput` validates the discount code and announces errors via `liveRegion`, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element (`_inputEl`) when validation fails. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 as the error state is not programmatically linked to the input field.

issuesFound.title: Carousel play button missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: In `testimonialsCarousel.init`, the `play()` function (which sets `aria-pressed="true"`) is only called if `autoPlay` is enabled. If `autoPlay` is false, the play button (`_playBtn`) is rendered without an initial `aria-pressed` attribute, leaving its state undefined for assistive technology on page load.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function in `statCounterAnimator` dynamically updates the text content of stat elements (`.stat__number`) to count up values. While it updates `aria-label` on completion, it does not use a live region to announce the change to screen reader users who are not focused on the element when the animation occurs.

issuesFound.title: Cookie preferences trigger missing aria-haspopup
issuesFound.explanation: The click handler for `[data-cookie-prefs]` in `cookieConsent` opens a dialog banner (`_showBanner`), but the trigger button is not updated with `aria-haspopup="dialog"` or `aria-expanded="true"`. Assistive technology users are not informed that activating the button will open a dialog.

issuesFound.title: File upload validation fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_addFile` function in `fileUploadField` validates files (size/type) and announces errors via `liveRegion`, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the native file input (`inputEl`) or the drop zone (`zoneEl`) when validation fails. This prevents assistive technology from identifying the specific field in error.

issuesFound.title: Wizard Previous button missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: In `multiStepWizard._updateNavButtons`, the Previous button (`_prevBtn`) controls the visibility of step panels but lacks an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the step container (`_stepsContainer`). The relationship between the navigation control and the content it manipulates is not programmatically determinable.

issuesFound.title: Wizard Next button missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: In `multiStepWizard._updateNavButtons`, the Next button (`_nextBtn`) controls the visibility of step panels but lacks an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the step container (`_stepsContainer`). The relationship between the navigation control and the content it manipulates is not programmatically determinable.

fpTitles: Wizard Previous button missing aria-controls
Wizard Next button missing aria-controls

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed when interacting with the toggle.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1705: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1735: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialisation does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init()` function locates the trigger element but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute. If the HTML markup lacks this attribute, the initial state is unknown to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1815: if (_trigger && !_trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` functions manage the panel visibility but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`inst.trigger`). This breaks the disclosure pattern for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1965: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1985: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` function expands the answer panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger (`item.trigger`). The `close()` function correctly sets "false", creating an inconsistent state where opening is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2755: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.mount()` function configures the search input with `role="combobox"` and `aria-controls` but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute. The attribute is only added when suggestions are shown, leaving the initial state undefined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2445: setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid()` function removes the `aria-describedby` reference when a field becomes valid but fails to remove the `aria-invalid="true"` attribute. This causes screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid even after the error is resolved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3555: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateCountBadge()` function updates the text content of the product count badge when filters are applied, but does not announce this change via an ARIA live region. Users relying on screen readers will not know the number of results has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3055: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing price update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._updatePlanCard()` function updates the visible price text when the billing period changes, but does not announce the new price or period via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the dynamic price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3860: liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target} ${priceFormatter.periodLabel(period)}`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `scrollToTop._onClick()` function scrolls the page to the top and moves focus, but does not announce the action via an ARIA live region. While focus moves, an explicit confirmation improves orientation for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205: liveRegion.announce('Scrolled back to top of page');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button (_trigger) is toggled visually, but its aria-expanded state is never updated in the open() or close() functions. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open(): add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after addClass(_drawer, ...).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped inside it. Keyboard users can tab out of the menu into the underlying page content, which is visually obscured but still focusable.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.open(): add if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate(); before liveRegion.announce.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function marks a field as invalid visually and adds a description, but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Assistive technologies rely on this attribute to announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markInvalid(): add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); before addDescribedBy.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the utility removes the error description but does not remove or reset aria-invalid. The field may remain announced as invalid to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: srUtils.markValid(): add field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); before removing aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated to "true" when the answer panel opens. Screen readers will incorrectly report the answer as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: faqAccordion.open(): add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after addClass(item.el, CSS.open).

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce changes to live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, prices update visually but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are unaware that the prices have switched to monthly or annual.
issuesFound.fix: Line: pricing.setPeriod(): add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingMonthly or STRINGS.pricingAnnual); after _updateSavingsMessage(period).

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters does not announce result count
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge updates, but screen reader users are not notified of the new number of results. They may think the page is static.
issuesFound.fix: Line: productFilter.applyFilters(): add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); inside the filterAnimation.run onDone callback.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input aria-expanded not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but aria-expanded is not set to "false" on initial mount. It only gets set when the list shows/hides. Initial state should be explicit.
issuesFound.fix: Line: suggestionListRenderer.mount(): add setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after setting aria-haspopup.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button aria-pressed not synced on init
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button's aria-pressed state is only updated in play()/pause() functions. If the carousel auto-plays on init, the button state might not reflect "playing" immediately.
issuesFound.fix: Line: testimonialsCarousel.init(): add if (_playing) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true'); after play() call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (`.nav-toggle`) changes state (open/close) but the JavaScript functions `open`, `close`, and `init` do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute. Screen reader users are not informed whether the menu is currently expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` toggle the panel visibility but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Assistive technologies cannot detect the open/closed state change.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion` `open` function expands the answer panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` and `markValid` helper functions manage error descriptions but do not toggle the `aria-invalid` attribute on the form field. Screen readers rely on `aria-invalid="true"` to announce that a field has an error.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge` function updates the visible product count text and `aria-label` but does not announce the change to a live region. Screen reader users filtering products are not informed of the new result count.

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateSavingsMessage` function dynamically updates the savings text when the billing period changes but does not announce this change via a live region. Users are not alerted to the new discount information.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input aria-expanded not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.mount` function sets up ARIA attributes for the combobox input but fails to set the initial `aria-expanded="false"`. Screen readers may not correctly identify the initial closed state of the suggestion list.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator text updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function updates the text content of statistic counters dynamically. The element lacks `role="status"` or `aria-live`, and no live region announcement is made, so screen reader users miss the counting animation or final value change.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions toggle CSS classes and scroll locking but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `_trigger` element. Screen reader users will not be informed when the navigation menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1383: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1403: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init()` function sets up event listeners but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the `_trigger` element. Assistive technologies will not know the menu is closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1433: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer opens without focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init()` function creates a `_trap` variable but never initialises it with `createFocusTrap(_drawer)`. When the menu opens, keyboard focus can escape the drawer to the rest of the page, violating focus containment requirements for modal-like dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1433: _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: true });

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` functions show/hide the panel but do not update `aria-expanded` on the `inst.trigger` button. Screen reader users will not receive state changes for dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1533: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1548: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu.register()` function sets `aria-haspopup` and hides the panel but fails to set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on the trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1583: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid()` utility function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid `field` element. Assistive technologies may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2853: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates without aria-live announcement

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (`_drawer`) opens as a modal-like overlay but does not trap keyboard focus. Users can tab out of the menu into the background content. The `_trap` variable is declared but never instantiated or activated.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons (`trigger`) toggle the visibility of panels, but `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` do not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger. `register()` also fails to initialize the attribute. Screen reader users are not informed of the dropdown state.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` in `init()`, but `_syncToggleState()` only toggles a CSS class. It never updates `aria-checked`. Screen reader users perceive a switch that never changes state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2575: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));
  Line 2630: if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period selection buttons (`_periodBtns`) act as a toggle group but only receive a CSS class update in `_syncToggleState()`. They lack `aria-pressed` to indicate which period is currently selected to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2575: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons (`[data-filter-value]`) inside `_tabsEl` toggle product visibility but only receive a CSS class update in `_syncTabAriaStates()`. They lack `aria-pressed` to indicate the active filter to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge updates not announced to screen readers

Line 2245: _countBadge.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message updates not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.fix:
Line 2610: _savingsMsg.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`_inputEl`) acts as a combobox trigger for the suggestion list. `_open()` and `_close()` update internal state but do not update `aria-expanded` on the input. `init()` also fails to set the initial state. Screen reader users don't know if suggestions are visible.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent trigger button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie preferences button (`[data-cookie-prefs]`) opens a dialog (`_showBanner`), but the trigger's `aria-expanded` state is never updated in `_showBanner` or `_onPrefsClick`. Screen reader users are not informed that the preferences dialog is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded state

issuesFound.fix: Line ~2235 (open function): setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed state

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message updates are not announced

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input aria-expanded not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox trigger but `aria-expanded` is not initialized to "false" on page load. It is only managed during interaction, leaving the initial state undefined for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~985 (mount): setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The email input in NewsletterForm references 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby, but no element with this ID exists in the DOM. The ErrorMessage component is rendered with ID 'newsletter-status' instead, leaving screen reader users without programmatic access to field-specific validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1677: aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-status' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Modal trigger disconnected causing focus loss
issuesFound.explanation: LuminaryPage passes onRequestDemo and demoTriggerRef to SiteHeader, but SiteHeader does not accept or use these props. Consequently, no element receives the ref, and when the Modal closes (Line 2343), focus cannot be restored to the trigger, violating WCAG 3.2.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2335: <SiteHeader items={items} onRequestDemo={openDemoModal} demoTriggerRef={demoTriggerRef} /> (Note: Requires updating SiteHeader interface and implementation to render a button that accepts demoTriggerRef and calls onRequestDemo)

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup missing errorId and errorMessage props
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects `errorMessage` and `errorId` props to render the error message element. NewsletterForm passes `error` instead of `errorMessage` and omits `errorId`. Consequently, the error message is never rendered, and the TextInput's `aria-describedby` reference points to a non-existent ID, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1230: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} errorId="newsletter-email-error">

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox has an explicit width and height of 20 px, which is below the 24 px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.5 (Target Size). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating such a small control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size — search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button measures 28 px × 28 px, falling between the 24 px minimum and the 44 px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This may make activation difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 790: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — stats label text
issuesFound.explanation: The stat label uses rgba(255,255,255,0.75) (75 % opacity white) against a gradient background starting at #005fcc. The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 3.5:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (14 px) under WCAG 1.4.3 (Contrast Minimum).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210: `.stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — newsletter paragraph text
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter paragraph uses rgba(255,255,255,0.8) against a blue-purple gradient background. The reduced opacity lowers the contrast below the 4.5:1 threshold required for normal text, making it difficult for users with low vision to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1460: `.newsletter-section > p { font-size: var(--font-size-base); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-align: center; max-width: 520px; margin-inline: auto; margin-bottom: var(--space-8); padding-inline: var(--space-6); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative — footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links rely solely on colour (var(--colour-neutral-400)) to distinguish them from surrounding text, with text-decoration: none and no border, font-weight change, or other non-colour visual indicator in the default state (WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative — footer address links
issuesFound.explanation: Address links in the footer use text-decoration: none and rely only on colour changes for identification. Without an underline, border, or other visual cue in the default state, users cannot distinguish links from static text (WCAG 1.4.1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1510: `.footer-brand address a { color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); display: inline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative — footer bottom links
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer bottom section have text-decoration: none and no non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) in their default state, violating WCAG 1.4.1 by relying solely on colour for link identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1540: `.footer-bottom > p a { color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative — social media links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer remove default underlines with text-decoration: none and provide only colour feedback on hover. In their default state, they rely on colour alone to indicate they are interactive, which fails WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without providing a visible alternative such as `box-shadow` or a contrasting border. Keyboard users cannot perceive which button currently has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on primary navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The `#nav-menu > li > a` selector defines hover states but lacks a `:focus-visible` rule. It inherits `outline: none` from the global `:focus-visible` reset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating the main menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 477: `#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on navigation dropdown buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `#nav-menu > li > button` selector (used for dropdown toggles) has no `:focus-visible` style defined, inheriting the global `outline: none`. Keyboard users cannot see when these controls are focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 477: `#nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on FAQ content links
issuesFound.explanation: The `.faq-item dd p a` selector sets color but no focus indicator. Without a specific `:focus-visible` override, these links lose focus visibility due to the global `outline: none` reset.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1182: `.faq-item dd p a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer bottom links
issuesFound.explanation: The `.footer-bottom > p a` selector lacks a `:focus-visible` style, causing focus to be invisible when users tab to these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1299: `.footer-bottom > p a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]` selector sets explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24×24px minimum target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1260: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size for search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The `#site-search button[type="submit"]` selector sets width and height to 28px with zero padding, falling below the 44×44px recommended target size and providing no compensatory spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 572: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `overflow: visible`, which makes content visible instead of hiding it visually. Content intended only for assistive technologies becomes visible to all users, breaking layout and potentially causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The `.skip-link` class uses `transition: transform var(--transition-base)` for the slide-in animation, but no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule exists to disable this movement. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` class includes `transform` in its transition property for the active-state press effect, but lacks a reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement when users prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 732: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for product cards
issuesFound.explanation: The `.product-card` class transitions on `transform` for a hover lift effect, but no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block disables this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1063: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-ctas .btn-primary` selector includes `transform` in its transition for a hover lift effect, without a reduced-motion safeguard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 699: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a` selector sets `text-decoration: none` and the hover state only changes color, providing no non-color visual indicator (such as underline, border-bottom, or background change) to distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 595: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a` selector removes underlines and relies solely on color change (`color: var(--colour-neutral-0)`) on hover, failing to provide a secondary visual cue for users with color vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1293: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Root font size below recommended 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` selector sets `font-size: 12px`, making default body text smaller than the recommended 16px minimum for comfortable reading. Users with low vision may need to zoom more frequently to read content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 108: `html { font-size: 16px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating the page will not see which element has focus unless individual components override this style.
issuesFound.fix: Line 356: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline without providing a visible replacement. Combined with the global outline removal, buttons will have no focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size for search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size and below the 24px minimum. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 883: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to select the checkbox.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1730: width: 24px; height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size for footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a minimum height of 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the recommended 44px touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1759: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and does not visually hide content. This utility class is intended to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers, but as defined it displays content normally, breaking the intended accessibility pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 367-377: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01). The existing prefers-reduced-motion override only covers the base .pricing-card:hover, not this featured variant.

issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote element uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 323: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for table not-included cells
issuesFound.explanation: The selector for table cells with aria-label="Not included" or "Not available" uses color: var(--colour-neutral-300) which is #cbd5e1. On the white or zebra-striped backgrounds, this results in insufficient contrast for text content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1560: color: var(--colour-neutral-500);

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change (from neutral-700 to brand-primary) to indicate hover state. Users who cannot perceive color differences will not be able to identify these as interactive links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 837: (add to existing rule) text-decoration: underline; OR Line 842: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { text-decoration: underline; color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Body text font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which sets the rem unit to 12px. The body text inherits var(--font-size-base) which is 1rem (12px), falling below the 16px recommended minimum for body text. This impairs readability for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 281: font-size: 16px; (or remove the font-size declaration to use browser default)

issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none but does not provide a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible and 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Visually hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto instead of position: absolute and 1px dimensions. This makes supposedly screen-reader-only content visible to all users, cluttering the visual interface and potentially exposing content that should be hidden.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and visible dimensions (width: auto, height: auto), which fails to visually hide content. Content intended only for screen readers will be visible in the layout, causing visual clutter and confusion. A correct implementation requires position: absolute and 1×1 pixel dimensions with clipping.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transition for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition including transform (perspective rotation) and a hover effect with transform. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 782 is empty, failing to disable the animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .logo-list img
issuesFound.explanation: Logo list images have a transition on the filter property. While the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 917 sets animation: none, it does not disable the transition property, which still animates for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .step
issuesFound.explanation: The step class has a transition property affecting box-shadow and border-color. While these are less dramatic than transform, the transition still constitutes animation that should be disabled for users who prefer reduced motion. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override for this selector.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making it difficult for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities to distinguish word boundaries. WCAG 1.4.12 (Text Spacing) recommends allowing users to adjust spacing, but negative values actively impede readability.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the default focus indicator for keyboard users across all interactive elements, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have a transform transition that may cause motion sensitivity issues, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Toast notification auto-dismisses without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toast component automatically removes notifications after a configured duration (dur) using setTimeout, but provides no mechanism to pause, stop, or hide this update on mouse hover or keyboard focus. Users with cognitive disabilities or those using screen magnification may not have sufficient time to read the notification before it disappears, violating WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer implements modal behavior by activating a focus trap and preventing interaction with background content, but it does not declare aria-modal="true" on the drawer element. Screen reader users may not be aware that the content outside the drawer is inert and cannot be interacted with while the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar missing required ARIA properties
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element has role="progressbar" but the JavaScript only sets aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext. It fails to set aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax, which are required properties for progressbar elements. Without these, screen readers cannot correctly calculate or announce the percentage of completion.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons have conflicting ARIA states
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons simultaneously declare both aria-pressed (indicating a toggle button state) and aria-selected (indicating a selected item in a list). These attributes are mutually exclusive and create conflicting information for assistive technologies about the control's nature and current state.

issuesFound.title: HTML lang attribute not set from configuration
issuesFound.explanation: The application configuration defines a locale (CONFIG.app.locale: 'en-GB') but the JavaScript never applies this value to the document's HTML element via the lang attribute. This prevents screen readers from selecting the correct pronunciation rules and language-specific behaviors for the page content, violating WCAG 3.1.1 (Language of Page).

issuesFound.title: Discount code input status not programmatically associated
issuesFound.explanation: The discount code input field displays validation status messages in a separate status element (_statusEl), but this element is not associated with the input via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage. Screen reader users may not be notified of error messages or success confirmations when they are displayed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not hide background content from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the code activates a focus trap but does not set aria-hidden="true" (or inert) on the main page content and header elements. Screen reader users can still navigate to background content using virtual cursor, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) as the background is not truly modal.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2980: After `_trap.activate();`, add code to set aria-hidden on the main content:
    Line 2980: `const main = qs(SELECTORS.main); if (main) main.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`
    Line 2981: `const header = qs(SELECTORS.header); if (header) header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`
    And remove these attributes in the close function after `_trap.deactivate();`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks required dialog role and aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element (`_drawer`) is treated as a modal dialog (focus trap, escape to close) but the JavaScript does not ensure it has `role="dialog"` or `aria-modal="true"`. These attributes are required for screen readers to announce it as a modal and prevent virtual cursor escape.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2979: After `_open = true;`, add:
    Line 2979: `if (_drawer) { setAttr(_drawer, 'role', 'dialog'); setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true'); setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-label', 'Navigation menu'); }`

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner does not hide background content from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner creates a focus trap but fails to set `aria-hidden="true"` on the page content behind it. While the banner has `aria-modal="true"`, without hiding the background, screen reader users can still perceive and navigate to the underlying page content, violating modal dialog accessibility requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5695: After `trap.activate();`, add:
    Line 5695: `const main = qs(SELECTORS.main); if (main) main.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`
    Line 5696: `const header = qs(SELECTORS.header); if (header) header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`
    And remove these in `_hideBanner` before releasing the trap.

issuesFound.title: Contact form personal data inputs lack autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for full name, email, company, and message are identified by selectors containing personal data signals (e.g., `[name="full_name"]`, `[name="email"]`), but the JavaScript does not programmatically add the `autocomplete` attribute to these inputs. This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from correctly identifying the purpose of these fields, violating WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5369: After `const field = qs(cfg.selector, _formEl);`, add:
    Line 5370: `if (field && cfg.selector.includes('name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`
    Line 5371: `if (field && cfg.selector.includes('email')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`
    Line 5372: `if (field && cfg.selector.includes('company')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'organization');`
    Line 5373: `if (field && cfg.selector.includes('message')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'off');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription email input lacks autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The email input (`#newsletter-email`) is a personal data field identified by type="email" and ID containing "email", but the JavaScript does not ensure it has `autocomplete="email"`. This prevents password managers and assistive technologies from correctly identifying the field purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5238: After `_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl);`, add:
    Line 5239: `if (_emailEl && !_emailEl.getAttribute('autocomplete')) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard personal data inputs lack autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard fields for name, email, and cardholder name (`[name="wizard_name"]`, `[name="wizard_email"]`, `[name="wizard_card_name"]`) contain clear personal data signals in their name attributes, but the JavaScript does not add appropriate autocomplete attributes (e.g., "name", "email", "cc-name") when binding validation to these fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5550: After `if (field) inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules);`, add:
    Line 5551: `if (cfg.selector.includes('wizard_name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`
    Line 5552: `if (cfg.selector.includes('wizard_email')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`
    Line 5553: `if (cfg.selector.includes('wizard_card_name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'cc-name');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion container lacks aria-multiselectable when multiOpen enabled
issuesFound.explanation: When the FAQ accordion is configured with `multiOpen: true`, the JavaScript does not set `aria-multiselectable="true"` on the container element (`_containerEl`). This attribute is required for screen readers to convey that multiple items can be expanded simultaneously in an accordion widget.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4780: After `_initialised = true;`, add:
    Line 4781: `if (_cfg.multiOpen) setAttr(_containerEl, 'aria-multiselectable', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing module does not announce price changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/annual), the JavaScript updates the price display elements visually and announces "Billing period changed to...", but it does not announce the actual new price values that appear on the page. Screen reader users navigating to the price elements later will hear the new values, but users who toggle the switch receive no confirmation of the price change amount, failing to provide equivalent programmatic notification of the content change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3954: After `liveRegion.announce(...)` for the period change, add announcement of the new price:
    Line 3955: `const priceText = plan.priceDisplay ? plan.priceDisplay.textContent : '';`
    Line 3956: `if (priceText) liveRegion.announce(`New price: ${priceText} per ${period}`);`

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without user control mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager.show() function sets a timer to auto-dismiss toast notifications after a fixed duration (dur) without providing a mechanism for users to pause, stop, or extend the time. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable) as users with cognitive disabilities or those who need more time to read may miss the content before it disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3380-3382: Replace the setTimeout with a user-controllable timer that pauses on hover/focus and resumes on leave, or provide a way to extend the time. Add clearTimeout(timerId) when the toast receives mouseenter or focus events, and reschedule on mouseleave or blur.

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input missing autocomplete attribute

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete attribute

issuesFound.title: Wizard form name input missing autocomplete attribute
