issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button may lack accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected via qs(SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn) but the code does not verify or ensure that the button has an accessible name (aria-label, aria-labelledby, or text content). If the HTML contains only an icon without text or aria-label, screen reader users will not know the button's purpose, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4494: After selecting _btn, add if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_btn.textContent.trim()) { _btn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top'); }.

issuesFound.title: Social links may lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The socialLinks selector targets '.social-links a' but the code does not ensure these anchor elements have accessible names. If the links contain only icon images without alt text or aria-label, screen reader users will hear only the URL or "link", violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 2.4.4.
issuesFound.fix: Line 356: In the initialization code (e.g., in srUtils.annotateExternalLinks or a dedicated social links init), add a check to ensure each social link has an aria-label describing the platform (e.g., 'Facebook', 'Twitter') if text content is missing.

issuesFound.title: Cookie settings button may lack accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie settings button is selected via '#cookie-settings-btn' but the code does not ensure this button has an accessible name. If the HTML contains only an icon or ambiguous text, screen reader users will not understand its purpose, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 357: In the initialization code (e.g., cookieConsent.init or a related module), add a check to ensure the button has an aria-label such as "Manage cookie preferences" if text content is missing.

Contact form email input missing autocomplete attribute
Contact form company input missing autocomplete attribute
Newsletter email input missing autocomplete attribute
Wizard form name input missing autocomplete attribute
Wizard form email input missing autocomplete attribute
Scroll-to-top button may lack accessible name

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function toggles the mobile navigation drawer but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen readers will continue to announce the button as "collapsed" even when the menu is open. The paired `close` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2191: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment inside the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the `_openInstance` function removes the `hidden` attribute and adds CSS classes but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the panel is expanded and that they can navigate into it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2144: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside the `_openInstance` function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function for FAQ items animates the panel open and removes the `hidden` attribute, but it never updates `aria-expanded` to "true" on the trigger button. While the `close` function correctly sets it to "false", the missing "true" value means the state is never communicated to assistive technologies when expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2934: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` utility function links the error message to the field via `aria-describedby` but omits setting `aria-invalid="true"`. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot programmatically identify that the field contains a validation error, breaking the form validation feedback loop for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1454: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the `markInvalid` function, before the `addDescribedBy` call.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause button missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button uses `aria-pressed` to indicate state, but this attribute is only set when the `play()` or `pause()` functions are called. When the carousel initializes with auto-play disabled (the default), the button never receives an initial `aria-pressed="false"`, leaving its state ambiguous to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3137: After `_playBtn = qs('[data-carousel-play]', _rootEl);`, add `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing));` to initialize the attribute based on the initial `_playing` state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function toggles the mobile navigation drawer open but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", the open() function omits the corresponding true state, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens dropdown panels but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The paired _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never communicated to assistive technologies when the dropdown opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` in the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When expanding a FAQ item, the function animates the panel and sets internal state but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", meaning the state is only communicated when closing, not when opening.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons toggle visual active states via CSS classes but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the pressed state to screen reader users. This prevents users from knowing which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` in the _syncTabAriaStates function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid form fields. Without this attribute, screen reader users cannot identify which fields contain errors when navigating through the form.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` in the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the mobile navigation drawer, the function deactivates the focus trap but fails to return keyboard focus to the trigger button that opened it. This violates focus management requirements for modal dialogs/disclosures, leaving focus in an unpredictable location.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` immediately before the emit call in the close() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The function references a _trap variable for focus management but never initializes or activates a focus trap when the drawer opens. This allows keyboard focus to escape the modal drawer and move to background elements, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.3 for modal dialog behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` immediately after `_lockScroll();` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function announces "Navigation menu opened" via liveRegion.announce(), but the close() function lacks a corresponding announcement. Users navigating with screen readers receive confirmation when the menu opens but not when it closes, creating an inconsistent experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before the closing brace of the close() function.

fpTitles: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer and updates the internal _open state, but it never calls setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true') on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state when the menu opens, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. The companion close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` within the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in the dropdownMenu module reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". This prevents screen reader users from knowing the dropdown is open. The _closeInstance function correctly sets it to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the _openInstance function, after checking `if (inst.open) return;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the open() function animates the panel and sets internal state, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the accordion panel is expanded. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2400: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` within the open() function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds the error element to the field's aria-describedby, but it fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users. This affects all forms using inlineValidator (contactForm, newsletterForm, multiStepWizard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1250: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function body.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus management and references it in destroy(), but the open() function never initializes the trap via createFocusTrap() nor calls activate(). Consequently, keyboard focus is not confined to the mobile navigation drawer when open, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() fails to return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the close() function deactivates the focus trap (if it were active) and unlocks scroll, but it does not programmatically return keyboard focus to the trigger button that opened it. Users relying on keyboard navigation will lose their place in the tab order.

issuesFound.title: productFilter view toggle buttons lack aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter module updates aria-pressed on view-mode toggle buttons when they are clicked (_onViewToggle), but it does not initialize the attribute on page load. When the page loads with a saved view mode (grid or list), the buttons lack aria-pressed, so screen readers cannot indicate which mode is active.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips does not manage focus after chip removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a user activates the remove button on a filter chip, the chip is removed from the DOM, but focus is not programmatically moved to the next chip, the container, or the filter controls. This causes focus to be lost to the document root, forcing keyboard users to restart tab navigation from the beginning of the page.

fpTitles: mobileNav.close() fails to return focus to trigger
activeFilterChips does not manage focus after chip removal

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background page while the drawer remains open, causing focus to be lost or hidden behind the overlay.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" when the page loads and the menu is closed. Screen readers may not recognize the button as controlling an expandable region.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded via the open() function, the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button is not set to "true". Although the close() function sets it to "false", the open state is never communicated, leaving screen readers unaware that the panel is expanded.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons to reflect the current state. The register() function initializes it to "false", but it is never toggled thereafter.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons that change the visible product category, but the function only toggles CSS classes and does not update aria-pressed to indicate which filter is currently active to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains an invalid value.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation clears an error from a field, the function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, causing screen readers to continue reporting the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count changes and items are shown or hidden, but no announcement is made to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed how many products match the selected filter or that the content has updated.

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price values update visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users may not realize the prices have changed or what the new values are.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function discovers the trigger button (_trigger) and drawer elements but does not initialise the aria-expanded attribute to "false" on page load. Since the menu is closed by default, assistive technologies cannot perceive the initial state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2102: After _initialised = true; add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function declares a _trap variable for focus management but never initialises or activates a focus trap when the drawer opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the open mobile menu into the background page content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button (_trigger) does not have an aria-controls attribute referencing the drawer element (_drawer). Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the toggle button, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets item.open = true and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. Although the close function sets it to "false", the asymmetry means screen readers do not announce the expanded state when items are opened, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2931: After item.open = true; add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function in inlineValidator adds error classes and updates aria-describedby via srUtils.markInvalid, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Assistive technologies cannot identify which fields have failed validation, violating WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3280: After addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');
  Line 3284: After addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid); add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the visible product cards, count badges, and empty state messaging when filters are applied, but does not announce these changes via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified that the content has changed or how many results are now showing, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2804: After emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...); add:
  liveRegion.announce(totalMatches === 0 ? STRINGS.filterNoResults || 'No products match your filters' : (STRINGS.filterResults || 'Showing {n} products').replace('{n}', String(totalMatches)));

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function updates pricing display values when switching between monthly and annual billing, but does not announce the new prices or the fact that the billing period has changed via a live region. Screen reader users may not perceive that prices have updated, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager.show function creates toast notifications that automatically dismiss after a configurable duration (default 4000ms) via setTimeout, but provides no mechanism for users to pause, stop, or hide the toast before it disappears. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable and 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide for users who need more time to read notifications.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar missing role
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateProgress function updates aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext on the progress bar element, but does not ensure the element has role="progressbar". If the HTML markup lacks this role, the element will not be identified as a progress indicator to assistive technologies, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3486: After if (!_progressBar) return; add:
  if (!_progressBar.getAttribute('role')) setAttr(_progressBar, 'role', 'progressbar');

fpTitles: Toast notifications auto-dismiss without pause mechanism
multiStepWizard progress bar missing role

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The functions that open and close dropdown panels modify the panel's hidden state and CSS classes but do not synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the expanded or collapsed state. Screen readers cannot announce the current state of the dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on triggers
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the functions add and remove CSS classes and animate the panel height, but they fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing whether the answer is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line faqAccordion.open: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`
  Line faqAccordion.close: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open and close functions toggle CSS classes on the drawer and body, but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the navigation toggle button to indicate the current state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`
  Line mobileNav.close: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed or aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the active CSS class on filter tab buttons based on the selected category, but it does not set aria-pressed (for toggle buttons) or aria-selected (for tabs) to convey the active state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line productFilter._syncTabAriaStates: Replace the forEach body with:
  `const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` (or `aria-selected` if implementing a tablist pattern)

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic content updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the function updates the visible card count and emits a custom event, but it does not announce the updated result count to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technologies are not informed that the filter has been applied or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: After `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', { count: totalMatches, state: Object.assign({}, s) });`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched between monthly and annual, the prices update visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users may not realise the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion expand/collapse not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, no announcement is made to screen readers to confirm the state change. While aria-expanded should be set (see Issue 2), providing a polite announcement improves the user experience by confirming the action.
issuesFound.fix:
Line faqAccordion.open: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);`
  Line faqAccordion.close: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds the error message element to the field's aria-describedby, but it fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function removes the error message from aria-describedby, but it does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` immediately after the aria-describedby cleanup logic (after the if/else block)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not initialise aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it finds the trigger button but does not set aria-expanded="false" to indicate that the menu is initially closed. This violates the requirement that ARIA state attributes must reflect the initial state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: After `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not initialise aria-expanded="false"
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" on page load to indicate that the suggestions are initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init: After `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion triggers lack Enter/Space keydown handling
issuesFound.explanation: The keyboard event handler for FAQ items handles arrow keys for navigation but does not handle Enter or Space keys to toggle the expand/collapse state when the trigger has role="button". Users cannot activate the accordion with keyboard alone if the trigger is not a native button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line faqAccordion._onKeydown: Before `default: return;`, add:
  `case KEYS.ENTER:`
  `case KEYS.SPACE: {`
  `  e.preventDefault();`
  `  const item = faqRegistry.getAll().find(i => i.trigger === trigger);`
  `  if (item) toggle(item);`
  `  break;`
  `}`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initialises it with createFocusTrap. When the mobile menu opens, focus is not constrained to the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay. Additionally, focus is not returned to the trigger on close because the trap was never activated.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open: After `_lockScroll();`, add:
  `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
  `_trap.activate();`
  Line mobileNav.close: Change `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();` to `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }` (or keep the check but ensure _trap is assigned in open)

fpTitles: mobileNav trigger does not initialise aria-expanded="false"
faqAccordion triggers lack Enter/Space keydown handling

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons missing aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not initialize aria-expanded during registration, and the _openInstance and _closeInstance functions do not update the attribute when panels are shown or hidden. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the expand/collapse state of dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in dropdownMenu.register after setting aria-controls): `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`
  Line (in dropdownMenu._openInstance after inst.panel.removeAttribute): `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line (in dropdownMenu._closeInstance after removeClass): `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger buttons missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: While the accordion initializes aria-expanded correctly in init(), the open() and close() functions toggle the visual state and CSS classes but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current expanded or collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in faqAccordion.open after addClass): `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line (in faqAccordion.close after removeClass): `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox input missing aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element configured as a combobox initializes with role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox" but does not set aria-expanded="false" initially, nor does it update the attribute to "true" when the suggestion listbox opens in _open() or back to "false" when it closes in _close().
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in siteSearch.init after setting aria-haspopup): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`
  Line (in siteSearch._open after _searchState.open = true): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line (in siteSearch._close after _searchState.open = false): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid on validated fields
issuesFound.explanation: When field validation fails, the code adds error classes and links the error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. When validation passes, it does not clear the attribute or set it to "false". This prevents screen readers from identifying invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in inlineValidator._renderResult inside `if (result.valid)` block, after srUtils.markValid): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`
  Line (in inlineValidator._renderResult inside `else` block, after srUtils.markInvalid): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly, quarterly, annual) indicate the active selection via CSS classes but do not set the aria-pressed attribute to communicate the pressed state to assistive technologies. As these buttons act as a toggle group, aria-pressed is required to indicate which period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in pricing._syncToggleState inside _periodBtns.forEach): `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons function as a tab list (or selectable button group) but only toggle CSS classes to indicate the active filter. They do not set aria-selected="true" on the active tab or aria-selected="false" on inactive tabs, preventing screen reader users from knowing which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in productFilter._syncTabAriaStates inside forEach): `setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter changes not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not update aria-expanded on triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers toggle panels but do not programmatically set aria-expanded to reflect the open/closed state, violating the disclosure widget pattern required by WCAG 4.1.2.

  Line (in _closeInstance, after `if (!inst.open) return;`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the code adds CSS classes and animates the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Although the close function sets it to "false", the asymmetry leaves the attribute in an incorrect state when expanded.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes aria-describedby references but leaves aria-invalid in its previous state (likely "true"), causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid even after it becomes valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in markValid, at end of function): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter does not announce dynamic updates

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken error message reference
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component is passed the error message via the prop name `error`, but the component expects `errorMessage`. Additionally, the `errorId` prop is omitted, so the ErrorMessage element is never rendered with an ID. Consequently, when the TextInput sets `aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error"`, it references an element that does not exist in the DOM, breaking the accessible name/description for screen reader users when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1732: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} required>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field broken error message reference
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives `error` instead of `errorMessage` and lacks the `errorId` prop. The TextInput at line ~1857 sets `aria-describedby="contact-name-error"`, but no element with that ID exists because ErrorMessage was not rendered with an ID. Screen readers cannot announce the error message for this field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1845: `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email field broken error message reference
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives `error` instead of `errorMessage` and lacks the `errorId` prop. The TextInput sets `aria-describedby="contact-email-error"` pointing to a non-existent element, preventing screen readers from associating the error message with the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1868: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject field broken error message reference
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives `error` instead of `errorMessage` and lacks the `errorId` prop. The TextInput sets `aria-describedby="contact-subject-error"` referencing a non-existent element, breaking the accessible description for the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1891: `<FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field broken error message reference
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives `error` instead of `errorMessage` and lacks the `errorId` prop. The Textarea sets `aria-describedby` including "contact-message-error", but no element exists with that ID because ErrorMessage was not rendered with it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1914: `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={\`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.\`} errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm validation rules use incorrect property name
issuesFound.explanation: The validation rules passed to useField use the property name `fn` to define the validation function, but the ValidationRule interface expects `validate`. This causes validation to fail silently (rule.validate is undefined), preventing error messages from being generated and announced to users, blocking form submission feedback for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1725-1728: 
  ```
  const email = useField('', [
    { validate: (v) => v.trim().length > 0 ? 'Email address is required.' : undefined },
    { validate: (v) => /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(v) ? undefined : 'Enter a valid email address.' },
  ]);
  ```

issuesFound.title: ContactForm validation rules use incorrect property name
issuesFound.explanation: The validation rules for name, email, subject, and message fields use `fn` instead of `validate`. The useField hook calls `rule.validate(value)`, which is undefined, causing validation to fail silently. Users do not receive error feedback, and screen readers cannot announce validation errors because they are never generated.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1800: `const name = useField('', [{ validate: (v) => v.trim().length > 0 ? 'Full name is required.' : undefined }]);`
  Line 1801-1804: `const email = useField('', [{ validate: (v) => v.trim().length > 0 ? 'Email address is required.' : undefined }, { validate: (v) => /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(v) ? undefined : 'Enter a valid email address.' }]);`
  Line 1805: `const subject = useField('', [{ validate: (v) => v.trim().length > 0 ? 'Subject is required.' : undefined }]);`
  Line 1806-1809: `const message = useField('', [{ validate: (v) => v.trim().length > 0 ? 'Message is required.' : undefined }, { validate: (v) => v.length <= MSG_MAX ? undefined : \`Message must be ${MSG_MAX} characters or fewer.\` }]);`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria-modal="true"` attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div instead of the dialog element. According to ARIA specifications, `aria-modal` must be on the element with `role="dialog"` to correctly indicate to assistive technologies that content outside the dialog is inert. Its current placement on the backdrop fails to communicate modal behavior properly.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1943: Remove `aria-modal="true"` from the `<div className="modal-overlay"` element.
  Line ~1946: Add `aria-modal="true"` to the `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog"` element.

issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects an `errorMessage` prop to render the error message and associate it via aria-describedby, but NewsletterForm passes `error` instead. This prevents the error message from rendering and creates a broken aria-describedby reference pointing to a non-existent ID 'newsletter-email-error', leaving screen reader users unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1077: Change `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` and add `errorId="newsletter-email-error"`. Line 1088: Ensure `aria-describedby` references 'newsletter-email-error' when error exists.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm removes focused elements on submission success
issuesFound.explanation: When the form submits successfully, the entire form element is replaced by a success paragraph using a ternary operator. If the submit button had focus at submission time, focus is lost and reset to the document body, disorienting keyboard and screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 862-865: Replace the conditional rendering with visibility toggling or move focus to the success message. Add tabIndex={-1} to the success paragraph and call .focus() on it when status changes to success, or wrap the form in a div with aria-hidden={status === 'success'} and render the success message separately without removing the form from the DOM.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm removes focused elements on submission success
issuesFound.explanation: When the form submits successfully, the entire form element is replaced by a success paragraph using a ternary operator. If the submit button had focus at submission time, focus is lost and reset to the document body, disorienting keyboard and screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 908-910: Replace the conditional rendering with visibility toggling or move focus to the success message. Add tabIndex={-1} to the success paragraph and call .focus() on it when status changes to success, or wrap the form in a div with aria-hidden={status === 'success'} and render the success message separately without removing the form from the DOM.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls a submenu whose visibility is toggled by the `isOpen` prop, but it lacks `aria-expanded`. Screen readers cannot announce whether the submenu is currently expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons behave as toggle buttons where one is active at a time (managed by `activeFilter`), but they lack `aria-pressed`. Screen readers cannot convey which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual billing buttons toggle a persistent binary state (`value`), but neither has `aria-pressed`. Screen readers cannot indicate which billing period is currently selected.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button in the navigation uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to screen reader users. This prevents users from knowing whether the submenu is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 669: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button in the mobile navigation uses aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed. Screen reader users cannot perceive the visual state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 750: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button uses IconButton without the required aria-label prop, leaving the button unnamed for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1215: Add aria-label="Next testimonial": `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">`

issuesFound.title: TextInput component does not expose aria-invalid to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but only uses it for styling. It does not pass aria-invalid to the underlying input element, so screen readers cannot announce when a field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 476: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the input: `<input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Textarea component does not expose aria-invalid to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but fails to map it to aria-invalid, preventing screen readers from announcing the error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 493: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the textarea: `<textarea id={id} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Select component does not expose aria-invalid to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop for visual styling but does not apply aria-invalid to the select element, leaving screen reader users unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 516: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the select: `<select id={id} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} {...rest} >`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The email TextInput has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" but the FormGroup component is not passed errorId="newsletter-email-error", so the error message element is never rendered with that ID. This creates a broken reference that confuses screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1337: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form name input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The name input has aria-describedby="contact-name-error" but FormGroup is not provided the errorId prop, so the error message element does not exist in the DOM with that ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1381: Pass errorId="contact-name-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references "contact-email-error" via aria-describedby, but the FormGroup component lacks the errorId prop, resulting in a broken ID reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1394: Pass errorId="contact-email-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form subject input references non-existent error element

issuesFound.fix: Line 1407: Pass errorId="contact-subject-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Contact form message textarea references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea conditionally references "contact-message-error" via aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not passed errorId="contact-message-error", so the ID does not exist when errors occur.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: Pass errorId="contact-message-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button lacks aria-pressed, preventing screen reader users from determining that it is a toggle button and which state is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1067: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link uses the item.current boolean to apply a visual "current" class but does not add aria-current="page" to communicate the current page location to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link applies a visual current state class but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen reader users from identifying which page they are currently on.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When the Button component is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it renders a spinner and disables itself but does not set aria-busy="true" to inform screen readers that the button is processing an action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 443: Add aria-busy={isLoading}: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog aria-modal attribute on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the overlay div instead of the dialog element itself. This incorrectly marks the backdrop as modal rather than the dialog, potentially confusing screen reader users about which element is the active modal container.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1567: Remove aria-modal from the overlay div. Line 1570: Add aria-modal="true" to the dialog div: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation target not focusable
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link targets "#main-nav" but the nav element with that ID is not focusable (lacks tabindex). When keyboard users activate the skip link, the viewport scrolls but focus does not move to the navigation, breaking the expected keyboard navigation flow.
issuesFound.fix: Line 693: Add tabIndex={-1} to the nav element: `<nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav" aria-label="Desktop navigation" tabIndex={-1}>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks search target not focusable
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link targets "#site-search" which is a form element. Forms are not focusable by default, so activating the skip link does not move keyboard focus to the search form, requiring users to tab through intervening content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 832: Add tabIndex={-1} to the form element: `<form id="site-search" aria-labelledby={labelId} className="site-search-form" onSubmit={handleSubmit} tabIndex={-1}>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown chevron characters (▲ ▼) are decorative but are not hidden from screen readers. Since the button already has a text label, the chevron creates redundant noise for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 672: Add aria-hidden="true" to the chevron span: `{isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk (*) is rendered as a visual indicator but is announced by screen readers as "star" because it lacks aria-hidden. While a visually hidden "(required)" text is also provided, the asterisk creates unnecessary verbosity.
